hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: The property has value as an early-twentieth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of early-twentieth-century architectural styles. Through its construction date and typology, the property is directly associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s. The property has retained most of its original building features, including brick cladding with decorative stonework, gabled roofline, flat headed and segmentally arched fenestration, projecting first storey bay windows, and central front entrance with leaded glass sidelights. The east elevation, facing Bellevue Avenue, also features a side entrance with leaded glass sidelights. Contextually, the property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on both Oxford Street and Bellevue Avenue. The property supports and maintains the residential character of the streetscape and is functionally, visually, and historically linked to the District.

facade_description: 93 Oxford St presents a house-form, detached facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a gable, consistent with its house-form, detached typology. The facade reads primarily as brick, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 2 window bays from lower to upper levels, using double-hung sash windows, and 1 principal entrance. Character-defining facade elements include decorative brick, original windows, bay window. The east elevation, facing Bellevue Avenue, also features a side entrance with leaded glass sidelights.
